question: The professor suggested _____ the experiment once more to verify the anomalous results.
explanation: 'Suggest' is followed by gerund (V-ing) when no object is used.

question: The structural engineer was too busy _____ the blueprints to notice the error in the load calculation.
_____ the data carefully, the team discovered several inconsistencies in the initial readings.
explanation: Perfect participle (Having + V3) shows the action completed before the main verb.

question: The students preferred _____ the practical session rather than _____ the theoretical lecture.
explanation: 'Prefer' + gerund when contrasting with another gerund.

question: The new software seems _____ the processing time significantly in finite element analysis.
_____ the bridge under heavy traffic, the engineers decided to reinforce the pillars.
explanation: Perfect participle for prior observation leading to decision.

question: The manager objected to _____ the deadline without proper approval from the client.
The geotechnical report needs _____ thoroughly before finalizing the foundation design.
explanation: Passive infinitive required after 'needs' when the subject is acted upon.

question: _____ all safety protocols, the construction proceeded without any major incidents.
explanation: Perfect participle indicates completion before the main action.

question: The team is capable of _____ the project within the stipulated time frame.
She was the only student _____ to solve the complex differential equation during the exam.
explanation: Infinitive after 'only' in this superlative-like structure.

question: The contractor denied _____ any compromise in the quality of materials used.
explanation: Perfect gerund for action completed before the denial.

question: It is worth _____ the new seismic retrofitting technique for old buildings.
The machine stopped _____ suddenly due to overload.
_____ the risks involved, the investors still decided to fund the hydropower project.
explanation: Present participle for simultaneous action or reason.

question: The professor made the students _____ the entire derivation on the board.
We look forward to _____ from you regarding the tender submission.
The faulty wiring risked _____ the entire laboratory.
_____ early, the research team managed to complete the literature review on time.
The committee recommended _____ the proposal for further environmental impact assessment.
She seems _____ the concept of finite element method quite well.
The auditor insisted on _____ all financial documents before approving the budget.
_____ the survey data, the urban planner proposed several changes in the layout.
explanation: Perfect participle for completed prior action.

question: The students were made _____ the safety drills repeatedly.
explanation: Passive 'be made' takes full infinitive with 'to'.

question: It is no use _____ over the minor calculation errors at this stage.
The design failed _____ the required safety standards set by the authorities.
_____ by the positive results, the team proceeded with the next phase of testing.
explanation: Past participle as adjective showing passive state.

question: The engineer warned the workers against _____ near the unstable scaffolding.
We are planning _____ the conference on sustainable engineering practices next month.
The old machine needs _____ before it can be used in the new project.
_____ the instructions carefully is essential for accurate experimental results.
explanation: Gerund as subject of the sentence.

question: The team succeeded in _____ the prototype within the tight deadline.
The professor asked the class _____ the difference between stress and strain.
_____ such advanced topics requires years of dedicated study.
The building is reported _____ during the recent earthquake with minimal damage.
She is interested in _____ a career in environmental engineering.
The committee decided _____ the tender to the most qualified bidder.
_____ the model several times, the researchers finally obtained reliable data.
The supervisor prevented the interns from _____ the high-voltage equipment alone.
It is difficult _____ accurate predictions without sufficient historical data.
The new regulation aims at _____ the carbon emissions from construction activities.
The students were seen _____ the lab equipment after the practical session.
explanation: Present participle after verbs of perception for ongoing action.

question: He is capable _____ complex structural problems efficiently.
_____ the budget constraints, the project team still delivered quality work.
explanation: Present participle as preposition-like showing reason.

question: The machine is used _____ heavy loads in the workshop.
The dean encouraged the faculty _____ more research papers in reputed journals.
There is no point in _____ about the past mistakes in the design phase.

_____ the presentation, the speaker invited questions from the audience.
